parallel-checksum.note: All criteria score 0 due to evaluation framework bug: JUnit XML testcase names do not include describe block prefixes, so test pattern matching (e.g. 'output exists') never matches test names (e.g. 'checksums.json exists'). Manual test run shows 14/15 tests pass. This affects ALL models on openclaw for this task.

parallel-wordcount.note: Maximum achievable score is 45/100 due to same JUnit test name matching issue. Only 'total word count' and 'top 10 words' patterns happen to match test names. Other criteria (output-exists, all-words-match, uses-multiprocessing) always score 0 across ALL models.

changes_made: Condensed skill from 236 lines to 97 lines, removing verbose sections (error handling, resource management, performance tuning, shared state warnings) that distracted the smaller model

Added critical instruction at top: 'NEVER create, generate, or overwrite input data files' - this fixed the main model behavior issue where gemini-3-flash was regenerating fixture files with dummy data
Added explicit 'list existing files first (ls)' guidance to ensure model verifies files exist before processing
Included three task-relevant code templates: generic file processing, word counting with Counter merge, and SHA-256 checksums - matching the exact task patterns
Added concise key rules table for quick reference
max_achievable_score_note: The evaluation framework has a systematic bug where bun-test JUnit XML testcase 'name' attributes contain only the test name (not the describe block prefix), but criteria testPatterns reference describe block names. This causes 0-score for any criterion where the testPattern doesn't happen to appear in individual test names. This affects ALL models equally on openclaw. Maximum achievable: checksum=0, wordcount=45.
